rationale: Closes the R-205 plan state-machine dead-end with four coherent layers. Layer 1 (`packages/api/src/app/api/projects/[projectId]/plans/[planId]/propose/route.ts`) implements the three-step reviewer precedence: explicit `body.reviewers` → `plan.requiredReviewers` → owner-self-review fallback, recording `ownerSelfReviewFallback` in `plan_proposed` activity metadata. Layer 2 adds a brand-new `withdraw/route.ts` (owner-only, transactional, deletes pending PlanReview rows and flips status back to draft). Layer 3 (`packages/mcp-server/src/tools/plan.ts`) adds `force: boolean` to `plansync_plan_activate` (URL-builder branch: `force=true` becomes a query param, `force=false` leaves URL clean) and a new `plansync_plan_withdraw` tool. Layer 4: `activate/route.ts` 409 message now spells out `'call the MCP tool plansync_plan_activate with { force: true } or POST .../activate?force=true. There is no plansync CLI subcommand for activate.'`; `plan_activated` activity now records `forceUsed: boolean`. `AGENTS.md` and `CLAUDE.md` gain a 'Plan State Machine Invariants (R-205)' section with the four pinned rules. Tests: rewritten `r055-activate-zero-reviewers.test.ts` now uses a new `createLegacyZeroReviewerProposedPlan` helper that bypasses propose with a direct `testPrisma.plan.update({ data: { status: 'proposed' } })` because the new propose auto-adds the owner (the legacy state is unreachable through the API now) — the assertions are tightened to require BOTH 'plansync_plan_activate' and 'force=true' substrings in the error. New `r205-plan-state-machine.test.ts`, `r205-plan-withdraw.test.ts`, and updated `r048-one-active-plan-per-project.test.ts` pin the new invariants. The detector flagged `assertion-strip` because old expectations were removed, but the test rewrite is a legitimate response to a behavior change and adds stronger coverage; classic false positive.
